claim: Reframe optimization: instead of 'how to make engineers faster,' ask 'how to make agents faster'—this dismantles traditional Scrum ceremonies.
actionable: Shift team focus from engineer velocity to agent capability; evaluate tools and processes by whether they accelerate agent output.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Case study results: 2 engineers with agents shipped 5x/day, 10-engineer team shipped 1x/5 days; output (by ticket complexity) was 10x higher.
actionable: Pilot agent-assisted development on a small team; measure by ticket complexity, not line count or PR count.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Scrum artifacts eliminated: no standups (tickets auto-update from PR state), no sprint planning (estimates irrelevant), no sprint refinement (spec → LDD → tickets flow).
actionable: Automate ticket status from PR state; replace sprint planning with lightweight spec + design doc generation by agents.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Development flow: spec → agent interviews → lightweight design doc (LDD) → auto-generated tickets → PRs (no blocking between tickets).
actionable: Implement spec generation; use agents to create LDDs from specs; auto-generate non-blocking tickets from LDDs.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Code review split: agents handle style/naming/opinionated tasks; engineers focus on system design and architecture.
actionable: Offload variable naming, style feedback, and linting to agents; engineer reviews focus on design trade-offs.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: QA agent: spins up on staging post-merge, checks acceptance criteria against tickets, flags failures for auto-remediation.
actionable: Implement post-merge QA agents that validate acceptance criteria; enable self-healing by auto-generating fix PRs.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Engineer selection matters: curious engineers thrive; engineers needing prescriptive specs struggle in agent-assisted environments.
actionable: Start agent adoption with curious, self-directed engineers; provide training for engineers transitioning to this model.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF

claim: Skills encode team culture: abstract software patterns into composable agent skills; avoid importing other teams' opinionated skills.
actionable: Build skills that match your team's conventions (e.g., service-repository pattern for APIs); don't blindly import community skills.
source_title: Agents Don't Do Standups: Building the Post-Engineer Engineering Org — Mike Spitz, PFF
